cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the minimum number of moves a knight needs to go from a1 to h8 on a standard chessboard. Let me start by recalling how a knight moves. Now, the problem is to find the shortest path from (0,0) to (7,7) using knight moves. This sounds like a classic shortest path problem. I remember that algorithms like BFS (Breadth-First Search) are typically used for such problems because they explore all possible moves level by level, ensuring the first time we reach the destination is via the shortest path. Use BFS starting from (0,0) to explore all reachable squares, keeping track of the number of moves taken to reach each square. 3.
